goal: No plaintext secrets in Postgres — mailbox passwords and OAuth access/refresh tokens stored encrypted, decrypted only server-side at point of use.
why: Closes concerns.md HIGH-2: mail_accounts.password and onedrive_connections.access_token/refresh_token sit in plaintext today (accounts.ts:62,92; connections.ts:30-31,55-56), a live-credential exposure if the service-role key or a DB backup leaks.
success_criteria: mail_accounts.password is stored encrypted (iv:authTag:ciphertext); SMTP/IMAP send still works because MailAccountWithSecret decrypts at point of use (REQ-4).
onedrive_connections.access_token and refresh_token are stored encrypted; getValidAccessToken returns a usable plaintext token and refresh still works against Microsoft's endpoint (REQ-5).
Encryption key is sourced from ENCRYPTION_KEY env (32-byte base64, validated lazily), never committed; rotation path documented in secrets.ts.
Public accessors (MailAccount, listAccounts, Connection, listConnections) still expose no secrets — the existing secret/no-secret split is unchanged.
Existing plaintext rows are not broken: tolerant decryptSecret reads them verbatim and the next write re-encrypts (self-healing backfill, no orphaned data).
No new npm dependency added; crypto is Node built-in node:crypto.

tasks.title: Server-only AES-256-GCM secret crypto util + env key
acceptance_criteria: encryptSecret returns iv:authTag:ciphertext (base64 segments) with a random 12-byte IV per call; two calls on the same input differ.
decryptSecret(encryptSecret(x)) === x round-trips for any UTF-8 string.
decryptSecret of a non-encrypted value (legacy plaintext password or raw JWT) returns the input unchanged — tolerant read, no throw.
isEncrypted returns true only for the 3-segment base64 GCM shape (12-byte IV, 16-byte auth tag), false for plaintext.
getCryptoEnv throws a clear 'Invalid crypto configuration' error if ENCRYPTION_KEY is missing or not exactly 32 bytes base64-decoded.
tasks.action: Add getCryptoEnv() lazy zod accessor for ENCRYPTION_KEY (32-byte base64, refined) to env.ts mirroring getTavilyEnv. Create src/lib/crypto/secrets.ts using node:crypto. encryptSecret: 12-byte random IV, aes-256-gcm, append getAuthTag(), join base64 with colons. decryptSecret: tolerant — return verbatim if !isEncrypted, else decode/setAuthTag/decipher, let GCM auth failure throw. isEncrypted: exactly 3 base64 parts, iv 12 bytes, tag 16 bytes. Document format, server-only, rotation in header.

tasks.title: Encrypt mailbox passwords at rest in mail/accounts.ts
acceptance_criteria: After saveAccount, mail_accounts.password holds iv:authTag:ciphertext, not the raw password.
loadAccountWithSecret and loadAccountWithSecretByEmail return the original plaintext password (decrypt in the accessor).
listAccounts and the public MailAccount shape still expose no password field.
An existing plaintext-password row is still readable via tolerant decryptSecret, and the next saveAccount upsert re-writes it encrypted (self-healing backfill, no separate script).
tasks.action: Import encryptSecret, decryptSecret from @/lib/crypto/secrets. In saveAccount change accounts.ts:92 'password: fields.password,' to 'password: encryptSecret(fields.password),'. In toMailAccountWithSecret change accounts.ts:62 'password: row.password,' to 'password: decryptSecret(row.password),' (both load paths funnel through this mapper). Leave toMailAccount, listAccounts, and the MailAccount interface untouched. Add a one-line comment noting the self-healing backfill.

tasks.title: Encrypt OAuth access/refresh tokens at rest in microsoft/connections.ts
acceptance_criteria: After saveConnection, onedrive_connections.access_token and refresh_token hold iv:authTag:ciphertext, not the raw JWT/refresh string.

getValidAccessToken returns a usable plaintext token in both branches: fresh token is decrypted before return; expired path calls refreshTokens with the decrypted refresh token then persists the new pair encrypted.
refreshTokens receives a decrypted refresh token (Microsoft's endpoint never gets ciphertext).
A legacy plaintext-token row keeps working via tolerant decryptSecret and is re-persisted encrypted on the next saveConnection or refresh (self-healing backfill).
tasks.action: Import encryptSecret/decryptSecret. In saveConnection (:55-56) wrap access_token/refresh_token writes in encryptSecret. In getValidAccessToken: fresh branch (:112) return decryptSecret(row.access_token); refresh call (:114) refreshTokens(decryptSecret(row.refresh_token)); re-persist (:120-121) wrap next tokens in encryptSecret; final return next.accessToken (:128) stays plaintext. loadRow/toConnection/listConnections unchanged. Add self-healing-backfill comment.
